reasoning: Response returns Tajima Beef (maff_gi:2) as the first item with correct MAFF GI registration, immediately followed by Kobe Beef (maff_gi:3) which explicitly explains the Tajima–Kobe relationship. Both records are authoritative official translations at high confidence. The 195-item list includes other Hyogo GI foods unrelated to the query (Sayo Mochi Daizu, Awajishima fugu etc.) but the most relevant items are ranked first. One notable caveat: no visiting/tasting venue or restaurant data is included, limiting practical trip-planning use.

improvement_hint: Add a see_also_venues or restaurant cluster for top GI beef items so agents can answer 'where to eat' follow-ups without a second tool call

reasoning: Arabic query correctly served with lang=ar. Top items are Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef in Arabic, both from maff_gi with official_translated high-confidence. The Arabic descriptions are accurate and fluent. No constraint failure per RULE D — language matches query. Same minor caveat as L1-01 (no venue/eating-place data). RULE D not triggered because language does match.

improvement_hint: Same as L1-01: add see_also_venues cluster for flagship GI products to enable dining follow-up in the same language

The tool scope_note even warns that this tool does not cover the queried intent. This is a retrieval failure: the relevant entity class (rural tea landscapes, Minami-Yamashiro specific data) is entirely absent. Per RULE A the response is scored on what was returned — mostly high-heritage Kyoto city landmarks.

improvement_hint: Route tea-landscape/chagyo queries to get_spots with municipality filter or search_hybrid rather than get_japan_heritage; add municipality-level tea field entries for Minami-Yamashiro

reasoning: Query asks specifically about 中芸地域 (Nakayi region) yuzu. Tool returned 500 of 651 Kochi local food items. The second and many following items are scraped_local_food with irrelevant content (temples, tourism spots, a Giverny garden). The specific 中芸ゆず GI entity appears to be absent or buried beyond the truncation. The relevant item is present in the prefecture data but ranking puts irrelevant scraped content ahead of the queried yuzu product. Per RULE C this is authoritative-but-incomplete = 3 for intent.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず specific GI record (MAFF if exists) or explicit 中芸 region yuzu data; filtering by keyword '中芸' or '柚子' would narrow results

reasoning: English query about traditional pottery in Kyushu. get_local_specialty(Saga, craft) returns 3 METI craft items: Hakata Ori (weaving, not pottery but a traditional craft), Imari-Arita Ware (ceramic, directly relevant, UNESCO-adjacent), and Karatsu Ware (ceramic, tea ceremony famous). Two of three items directly answer pottery query. The scope is Saga only rather than all Kyushu — Kagoshima Satsuma ware and Kumamoto Amakusa pottery are absent. Per RULE C: authoritative data with geographic scope gap. see_also_wikidata_venues adds a library/museum cross-link. One caveat: Hakata Ori is weaving not pottery, slightly dilutes the crafts list but tool filtered to craft category correctly.

Returns only 2 local Aomori festival items: Hachinohe Sansha Taisai and Aomori Nebuta Matsuri, both with correct Korean translations and Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property designations. Data is accurate and useful for what it covers. Major gaps: only Aomori covered (no Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa — all famous Tohoku festivals); region_fanout_applied=false confirms no regional expansion was applied. national_heritage items are from Akita and Saitama (not Tohoku-specific).
